status: UAPKI-attributed, not independently confirmed against the paid official text
provenance: Transcribed from oracles/uapki/library/uapkic/src/dstu8845.c dstu8845_self_test() (commit c64181c3b1cd437139119d83bffb5ab090b1cdd6), whose source comments the block '// ДСТУ 8845:2019' - i.e. the library's own authors claim these are the standard's worked-answer values, not self-invented test inputs. UAPKI is a fork of Cryptonite (PrivatBank) and its README cites an official Ukrainian state crypto-expertise conclusion (No 04/05/02-2096, 2021) for the project generally - see ORACLES.md for the exact caveats on what that pedigree does and doesn't establish (the conclusion covers a specific certified build, not this cloned commit). This project has NOT independently confirmed these values against the official (paid) DSTU 8845:2019 text itself.
cross_check: Independently reproduced byte-for-byte by running oracles/strumok-dstu8845/ (outspace, a separate BSD-2-Clause C implementation) on the same key/IV inputs. Treat this as a consistency bonus, not independent-oracle confirmation: outspace and UAPKI's dstu8845.c share identical internal function/table names (dstu8845_init, dstu8845_crypt, T0..T7) - a strong signal of shared lineage rather than two people implementing from the spec independently (see DECISIONS.md D-15).
note: key/iv/keystream are the raw little-endian byte layout of the uint64_t[]/ByteArray buffers as consumed by dstu8845_init()/produced by dstu8845_crypt() in both reference implementations - not a DSTU-defined big-endian encoding. keystream is dstu8845_crypt() applied to an all-zero 64-byte input buffer, i.e. the raw keystream (crypt XORs with input).
